zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard le Sayre於1180年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可時，他的父親John Bradshaw de Sayer IV20歲，母親Emma de Harcourt8歲。他於大約1215年與Filia de Musgrave在英國藍卡爾布雷蕭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世在法國皮卡迪索姆，埋葬在法國皮卡迪索姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard le Sayre于1180年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可时，他的父亲John Bradshaw de Sayer IV20岁，母亲Emma de Harcourt8岁。他于大约1215年与Filia de Musgrave在英国藍卡爾布雷蕭结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世在法国皮卡迪索姆，埋葬在法国皮卡迪索姆。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Sayre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Sayer  .

variants.name: Sayer
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard le Sayre was born in 1180, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Bradshaw de Sayer IV, was 20 and his mother, Emma de Harcourt, was 8. He married Filia de Musgrave about 1215, in Bradshaw, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in Somme, Picardie, France, and was buried in Somme, Picardie, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard le Sayre est né en 1180, à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Bradshaw de Sayer IV, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Emma de Harcourt, avait 8 ans. Il a épousé Filia de Musgrave environ 1215, à Bradshaw, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé à Somme, Picardie, France, et a été enterré à Somme, Picardie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard le Sayre 1180 in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Bradshaw de Sayer IV 20 und seine Mutter Emma de Harcourt 8. Er hat um 1215 in Bradshaw, Lancashire, England Filia de Musgrave geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in Somme, Picardie, Frankreich und wurde in Somme, Picardie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard le Sayre è nato il nel 1180 nel Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Bradshaw de Sayer IV, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Emma de Harcourt, aveva 8 anni. Lui sposato Filia de Musgrave circa 1215, nel Bradshaw, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel Somme, Picardie, Francia, e fu sepolto a Somme, Picardie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard le Sayreが1180年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクで生まれた時，父John Bradshaw de Sayer IVは20歳，母Emma de Harcourtは8歳であった。約1215年にイギリスランカシュアイアブラドショーでFilia de Musgraveと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼はフランスピカーディソンメで死去し，フランスピカーディソンメにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard le Sayre(이)가 1180년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Bradshaw de Sayer IV(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Emma de Harcourt(은)는 8세였다. 그는 대략 1215년 영국 랭카실르 브래드쇼에서 Filia de Musgrave(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 프랑스 피카디 솜에서 사망했으며 프랑스 피카디 솜에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard le Sayre nasceu em 1180, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Bradshaw de Sayer IV, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Emma de Harcourt, tinha 8 anos. Ele casou-se com Filia de Musgrave aproximadamente 1215, em Bradshaw, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em Somme, Picardie, França, e foi sepultado em Somme, Picardie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard le Sayre родился в 1180 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Bradshaw de Sayer IV было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Emma de Harcourt, 8. Он женился на женщине по имени Filia de Musgrave около 1215 г. в местности брадшав, ланкашир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в местности сомм, пикарди, Франция и был похоронен в местности сомм, пикарди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard le Sayre nació en 1180, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Bradshaw de Sayer IV, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Emma de Harcourt, tenía 8 años. Se casó con Filia de Musgrave aproximadamente 1215, en Bradshaw, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en Somme, Picardie, Francia, y fue sepultado en Somme, Picardie, Francia.
